data.title: Rename --btn-accent to --accent; implement & document 3-tier border system

data.body:
Two parts (decisions finalized in design.md review):

(1) RENAME --btn-accent -> --accent. The CSS custom property is set on every page's <main> holding that page's accent color (house deep/light or section color) and read by button.tsx. It's no longer button-specific (borders will read it too), so rename to --accent. 8 page set-sites: NeighborhoodPage, StoryPage, LiberalArtsPage, LabPage, PoliticalClubPage, PeoplePage, CampusPage, EventsPage (each style={{ '--btn-accent': 'var(--color-house-X...)' }}). 5 reads in button.tsx (var(--btn-accent,...)). Rename ALL of them consistently to --accent. Keep --btn-fill/--btn-text button hover vars as-is (those ARE button-specific).

(2) 3-TIER BORDER SYSTEM — give borders clear defaults (user wants consistency; today there are scattered border-foreground/{10,20,40} + solid + faint + house). The three tiers:
 - STRUCTURAL (default frames/dividers): border-foreground-faint — the very light solid charcoal border. Already ~7 uses; keep.
 - DEFINITION / low-emphasis (on cream surfaces, definition without emphasis): border-foreground/20.
 - EMPHASIS / themed: var(--accent, currentColor) — the house accent color border, exactly like buttons use. Containers that want emphasis (the Visit page note container, the Events page embed) move to THIS tier (currently border-foreground/20 -> emphasis via --accent). Rationale (user): 'if we want emphasis, do the house accent color as the border the way the buttons do — they use whatever the foreground color is for each house.'
 Consolidate strays: Navbar border-foreground/10 and Hero solid border-foreground (combobox) -> structural faint or /20 (planner decides cleanest). border-foreground/40 STAYS as the Hero input focus-state ring/border (it's a state, not a resting border).
 Document the 3 tiers clearly in DESIGN.md (a borders section), and clarify the pill shape note already added.

VISUAL CHANGES — preview before merge. Acceptance: typecheck/build/conformance pass; tests at FRAC-199 baseline (7), no new failures; no --btn-accent references remain (all --accent).

REVIEW: PASS (independent, cold review). 

CRITICAL VAR-MATCH CONFIRMED: all 8 page <main> set-sites set --accent and button.tsx reads var(--accent,currentColor) at all 3 live sites (bg L52, border-color L55, nested hover:text L59). Names match — buttons are functionally identical, just renamed. grep -rn 'btn-accent' src/ DESIGN.md = ZERO. --btn-fill/--btn-text correctly retained (index.css:269, button.tsx:58-59) — NOT renamed.

4 BORDER EDITS, exactly as specified, no others touched:
- NeighborhoodPage:61 Visit Note /20 -> [border-color:var(--accent,currentColor)] (EMPHASIS) ✓
- EventsPage:47 Luma embed /20 -> [border-color:var(--accent,currentColor)] (EMPHASIS) ✓
- Hero:137 kbd-nav popover solid border-foreground -> border-foreground/20 (DEFINITION) ✓
- Navbar:397 menu divider /10 -> border-foreground-faint (STRUCTURAL) ✓
Both EMPHASIS sites keep leading 'border' utility + use the exact button pattern so they inherit each page's --accent. Focus /40 (Hero:198) and /60 (ArchiveSearch:79) states untouched; DocumentBadge/TagFilter hovers left as intentional flagged follow-ups.

DESIGN.md: new ### Borders subsection documents all 3 tiers (structural=faint, definition=/20, emphasis=var(--accent)) with accurate roles + example sites matching code; all 5 --btn-accent prose/YAML refs (L54,57,62,163,321) renamed to --accent. No stale refs.

GATES: typecheck PASS, build PASS, conformance PASS (35 grandfathered, green — var(--accent) not raw hex). Tests: 7 failed/148 passed = EXACTLY the FRAC-199 baseline, no new failures. The 2 buttons.test failures are pre-existing (aria-hidden corner count 5-vs-4 at L77; bg-[rgba(242,234,216,0.08)] drift at L135) — NEITHER is caused by the rename. The received className in L135's failure shows [border-color:var(--accent,currentColor)] correctly resolved, proving propagation works; the renamed propagation test (should propagate --accent) PASSES. neighborhood failures are ol/min-h-screen layout, unrelated to the Note border.

Diff scope clean: only the intended files (8 pages, button.tsx, buttons.test.tsx, Navbar, Hero, Campus comment, index.css comment, DESIGN.md). Visual preview gate remains for the human (2 emphasis-border changes). Moving to done.
